ocr.text: The analysis of Table 1 reveals key performance metrics with headers Parameter, PA vs WT, PA vs MEC, and WT vs MEC. The
Sensitivity Positive (SP) is 86.7% for PA vs WT, 82.1% for PA vs MEC, and 86.3% for WT vs MEC. The Sensitivity Negative
(SE) shows values of 82.2% for PA vs WT, 90.1% for PA vs MEC, and 84.8% for WT vs MEC. The Accuracy (ACC) measures
at 84.5% for PA vs WT, 86.2% for PA vs MEC, and 85.5% for WT vs MEC.
Parameter  PA vs WT  PA vs MEC  WT vs MEC
Parents% (n = 280)  Teachers% (n = 32)  Total% (n = 312)  χ2 #  df  p-Value
Adolescents like unhealthy foods  81.4 (228)  84.4 (27)  81.7 (255)  0.19  2  0.91
There is immense pressure from the school canteen personnel to sell unhealthy foods  47.5 (133)  18.8 (6)  44.6 (139)  14.31  2  <0.01
There is lack of any Indian government mandate regarding school canteen policy  63.9 (179)  43.8 (14)  61.9 (193)  5.42  2  0.67
The school management is reluctant towards the participation of student, teacher and parent in school canteen operations  52.9 (148)  15.6 (5)  49.0 (153)  16.60  2  <0.01
Students could probably violate the canteen policy rules  40.0 (112)  37.5 (12)  39.7 (124)  0.61  2  0.74
The Matthews correlation coefficient (MCC) has values of 0.689 for PA vs WT, 0.725 for PA vs MEC, and 0.710 for WT vs
MEC. The correlation coefficient (R) is recorded as 0.832 for PA vs WT, 0.812 for PA vs MEC, and 0.836 for WT vs MEC.
Table 2 presents survey results with headers indicating various categories relating to adolescents' food preferences, with
corresponding data for Parents% (n = 280), Teachers% (n = 32), Total% (n = 312), χ2 #, df, and p-Value. The result for
"Adolescents like unhealthy foods" shows 81.4 (228), 84.4 (27), and 81.7 (255) with χ2 = 0.19, df = 2, and p-Value = 0.91. The
statement regarding pressure from canteen personnel yields 47.5 (133), 18.8 (6), and 44.6 (139) with χ2 = 14.31, df = 2, and p-
Value < 0.01. The absence of a government mandate shows 63.9 (179), 43.8 (14), 61.9 (193) with χ2 = 5.42, df = 2, and p-Value
= 0.67. The reluctance of school management indicates 52.9 (148), 15.6 (5), 49.0 (153) with χ2 = 16.60, df = 2, and p-Value <
0.01. Lastly, the potential for students to violate canteen rules is represented by 40.0 (112), 37.5 (12), and 39.7 (124) with χ2 =
0.61, df = 2, and p-Value = 0.74.
